get.description: Retrieves reader component version information including Boot Loader, OS, Reader Application, and Radio Firmware versions
Retrieves reader network information including IP address, subnet mask, gateway and DNS

put.description: Updates the reader network configuration settings

get.description: Retrieves reader operational statistics for read points

Location.description: A URL that will provide a status of the restart

put.description: Restarts the reader with the device ID provided
Updates the GPO state on the specified port of the reader

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The GPO state signal to send

state.description:
desired GPO state

true: HIGH

false: LOW

put.description: Updates the application LED state on the reader to the state specified

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: The color of the LED (red, amber, green)

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: The duration in seconds for the LED state to be in effect

parameters.name: flash
parameters.description: A value indicating whether to flash the LED

get.description: Retrieves a value indicating the current state of the application LED

put.description: Update the OS software on the device

requestBody.description: The new reader configuration XML file

availableOsUpgrades.description: A List of available OS upgrades, (upgrade path only, no downgrade versions)

fpga.description: FPGA running on radio (only applicable to ATR7000)

message.description: A brief description of the error

parameters.description: A list of error parameters, if applicable

subnetMask.description: Subnet mask for the network adapter

dnsAddress.description: IP address of the DNS server

macAddress.description: MAC address of the reader (read only)

dnsAddress.description: IP address of the DNS server

subnetMask.description: Subnet mask for the network adapter

ipAddress.description: IP address of the reader (read only if dhcp = true)

readerflashmemory.v1.title: readerFlashMemory
readerflashmemory.v1.description: Non-volatile reader flash partitions and their usage information

offset.description: Local clock offset from NTP server clock in milliseconds.

reach.description: Status of successful synchronization attempts; a value of 377 indcates eight consecutive successful attempts.

uptime.description: Duration the reader has been powered on

systemTime.description: ISO 8601 formatted time on the reader

radioConnection.description: The status of the radio connection

0.description: Antenna 0 connection state (only applicable to ATR7000)

9.description: Antenna 9 connection state (only applicable to ATR7000)

10.description: Antenna 10 connection state (only applicable to ATR7000)

11.description: Antenna 11 connection state (only applicable to ATR7000)

12.description: Antenna 12 connection state (only applicable to ATR7000)

13.description: Antenna 13 connection state (only applicable to ATR7000)

powerSource.description: The source of power for the reader

powerNegotiation.description:
How the power supplied to the reader is negotiated
Only present if powerSource is POE or POE+

rootFileSystem.description: Root file system upgrade progress percentage

regionconfig.v1.title: regionConfig
regionconfig.v1.description: Represents the region and regulatory configuration.

lbtEnabled.description: A flag indicating whether listen before talk is enabled

inventorysettings.v1.title: inventorySettings
inventorysettings.v1.description: Reader settings for inventory mode. If absent, the reader will report each tag on each antenna once per minute.

interval.description: The time interval (how often) to report each tag

startTrigger.description: The GPI trigger to start reads on the portal

port.description: The GPI port to signal. If absent, port 1 is used.

signal.description: The signal value for the trigger. If absent, signal LOW is used

stopInterval.description: The interval at which to stop reads after the last unique tag is read (seconds). If absent, the reader will stop after not reading a unique tag in 3 seconds

tagidfilter.v1.title: tagIdFilter
tagidfilter.v1.description:
Represents filter on the tag id.

If absent, no filter is used.

value.description:
The value to match.


For prefix and suffix filters. Only hex digits are allowed and there must be an even number of hex digits.

When prefix filter is used, selects cannot be used. 

For regex filter, C++ STL regex values should be used.

match.description: The segment or method of the id to match.

signal.description: Transition to this GPI will trigger event

debounceTime.description: Time (in milliseconds) until GPI must remain at signal level to trigger event

mask.description: Mask Field (see EPC Gen2 Spec). The value must be a hex string (only values 0-9 and a-f) and must have an even number of characters.

truncate.description: Truncate Field (see EPC Gen2 Spec) If absent, truncation is disabled. If non-zero, the value indicates the number of bits to expect in the tag response.

delayBetweenAntennaCycles.v1.title: delayBetweenAntennaCycles
delayBetweenAntennaCycles.v1.description:
This introduces a delay between antenna cycles if no tags are read or if no unique tags are read. This allows the reader to share the spectrum if there are no tags to be read. 

If absent, 
on the ATR7000 and the FX9600, delayBetweenAntennas cycles is set to wait for 75 mS if no unique tags are read during a antenna cycle.  
on the FX7500, delayBetweenAntennas cycles is set to wait for 75 mS if no tags are read during a antenna cycle.

type.description: Condition under which to delay between antenna cycles

duration.description: Delay between antennas cycles in milliseconds to wait. Will only wait if the type (no tags or no unique tags) is met. If the type is DISABLED, duration must to be set to 0. IF the type is no DISABLED< the value must be set to a non-zero value.

tagMetaData.v1.title: tagMetaData
tagMetaData.v1.description:
Controls the metadata that is sent when a tag is reported

  “ANTENNA” will report the antenna port upon which the tag was inventoried.

  “RSSI” will report the rssi (in dbm) of the inventoried tag. If the tag is only reported occasionally (see reportFilter), this tag will be the peak rssi since the last reported read.

   "PHASE” will report the phase (in degrees) of the inventoried tag. This value will only be reported if each individual tag read is reported (in other words, if reportFilter duration is set to 0). Otherwise, it will not be reported.
  
  "CHANNEL” will report the channel (in MHz) the reader was using when the tag was inventoried. This value will only be reported if each individual tag read is reported (in other words, if reportFilter duration is set to 0). Otherwise, it will not be reported.
  
  “SEEN_COUNT” will report the number of times the tag has been inventoried since the previous report. This value will always be 1 if each individual tag read is reported (in other words, if reportFilter duration is set to 0). 
  
  "PC" will report the PC bits of the inventoried tag as a hex string. 
  
  "XPC" will report the XPC bits of the inventoried tag, if present, as a hex string. 
  
  "CRC" will report the CRC bits of the inventoried tag as a hex string.

  "EPC" will report the entire contents of the EPC bank as a hex string. If only a portion of the memory bank is desired, this can be requested by appending a [] to the string and placing the words requested. For instance, if only the first word is desired, the value can be set as "EPC[1]". If the first word and the 3-5 word are desired, the value can be set to "EPC[1,3-5]".
  
  "TID" will report the entire contents of the TID bank as a hex string. If only a portion of the memory bank is desired, this can be requested by appending a [] to the string and placing the words requested. For instance, if only the first word is desired, the value can be set as "TID[1]". If the first word and the 3-5 word are desired, the value can be set to "TID[1,3-5]".

  "USER" will report the entire contents of the USER bank as a hex string. If only a portion of the memory bank is desired, this can be requested by appending a [] to the string and placing the words requested. For instance, if only the first word is desired, the value can be set as "TID[1]". If the first word and the 3-5 word are desired, the value can be set to "TID[1,3-5]".
    
The array may also contain an object. The object must contain a single name value pair with the name being set to “userDefined” and the value being any desired string. Each time the tag is reported, this object will be included.


If absent,
“SIMPLE” mode does not report any additional meta data, “PORTAL” and “CONVEYOR” modes reports “ANTENNA”, and “INVENTORY” mode reports “ANTENNA”, “RSSI”, and “SEEN_COUNT”.

radioStartConditions.v1.title: radioStartConditions
radioStartConditions.v1.description:
Controls when, after a “start” is issued, the radio starts trying to inventory tags. 

If absent, the radio will immediately begin inventorying tags upon a "start" command.

radioStopConditions.v1.title: radioStopConditions
radioStopConditions.v1.description:
Controls when an ongoing operation completes. 

If absent, the radio will continue trying to inventory tags until a "stop" is issued.

duration.description: Time to run until radio stops (in seconds)

antennaCycles.description: The number of cycles through all enabled antennas before stopping the radio

tagCount.description: The number of tags to inventory until the radio stops. Cannot be set at the same time as durationAfterNoMoreUniqueTags

durationAfterNoMoreUniqueTags.description: Duration (in seconds) after not inventorying any more unique tags to stop the radio. Cannot be set at the same time as tagCount

tagReportFilter.v1.title: reportFilter
tagReportFilter.v1.description:
Controls when and how often a tag is reported

NOTE: This cannot be set while in "INVENTORY" mode. Setting the modeSpecificSetting for interval must be used in "INVENTORY" mode.

If absent, each mode uses a different default.

"SIMPLE": report tag read once.

"PORTAL" and "CONVEYOR": report each tag the first time it is read on each antenna.

duration.description:
Duration (in seconds) to wait to report a tag again once it has already been reported
It should be noted that the way the filter works is that as long as the tag is being read by the reader, it will not report unless the time since the previous report of this tag on this antenna meets the type and duration.

type.description: Configures the timeout (in seconds) by antenna or for entire radio

rssiFilter.v1.title: rssiFilter
rssiFilter.v1.description:
Tag with RSSI below threshold will not be inventoried by the radio

If absent, rssi filter is not used.

Note: Currently ONLY supported by the FX9600.

threshold.description: Threshold (in dbm) below which the radio will ignore a tag response

type.description: The type of mode of operation

environment.description:
The type of environment in which the reader operates. Along with the regulatory configuration of the reader, the environment parameter will set the default link profile parameters (i.e., Miller mode, BLF, Tari, etc.) and the receiver dynamic range (interference immunity).

  LOW_INTERFERENCE:  The reader is operating in an environment when the likelihood of interference is very low or only occurs for very brief periods of time (defined as a single interrogator environment in the Gen2 and ISO standards).

  HIGH_INTERFERENCE: The reader is operating in the presence of other readers (defined as a multi-interrogator or dense interrogator environment in the Gen2 and ISO standards).

  VERY_HIGH_INTERFERENCE: The reader is operating in an environment where the number of readers is greater than the number of available channels, or when interfering readers are in very close proximity to each other.

  AUTO_DETECT: This will cause the reader to try and assess the environment and adjust accordingly. 

  DEMO: Should be used when demonstrating the maximum performance (fastest read rate) of a reader. This assumes no other readers in the environement.

  If absent, the environment is set to HIGH_INTERFERENCE

antennas.description:
An array of integers representing the antenna ports to use to read tags. For ATR, the integers represent beam numbers. 

 If absent, all antennas ports are used; for the ATR, a set of 38 beams are used.

oneOf.description: Single value applies to all antennas

Each value applies to each antenna in the antennas array
Array length must match antennas array length

transmitPower.description:
Desired Transmit Power (in dbm).

 If absent, the value is set to 27 dbm; for the ATR, the value is set to 36 dbm EIRP.

oneOf.description:
Each value applies to each antenna in the antennas array
Array length must match antennas array length

antennaStopCondition.description:
Stop Condition for antennas. 

 If absent, the antenna stop condition is set to run a single inventory round for no longer than 1/N seconds. Where N is the number of enabled antennas.

oneOf.description:
Each value applies to each antenna in the antennas array
Array length must match antennas array length

query.description:
Gen2 query parameters. See Gen2 spec for details.

 If absent, "sel" is set to ALL, session is set to 1, target is set to A, and tag population set to 1.

selects.description:
Gen2 select parameters. See Gen2 spec for details.

 If absent, no select will be issued. Cannot be set when filter with prefix type is also set. 

 If there is an array of select objects, all of the selects in the array will be applied to all antennas.
 
 If there is an array of array of select objects, each array of select objects will apply to each antenna. The array of arrays must have the same number of entries as the antennas array.

delayAfterSelects.description: Duration in milliseconds to wait after issuing the final select before issuing a query. If absent, the minimum time will be used.

url.description: URL where the OS image is served

authenticationType.description: Type of authentication required to get OS image

username.description: User name required to access OS image

password.description: Password required to access OS image

actionBlink.v1.title: Action Blink Configuration
actionBlink.v1.description: action blink configuration for GPO or LED

ON.description: SET GPO or LED to desired state for ON (milliseconds) duration

OFF.description: Toggle GPO or LED for OFF(milliseconds) duration

DURATION.description: Total duration in milliseconds to perform blink. 0 will blink GPO or LED forever

conditionsArray.v1.title: Conditions Array
conditionsArray.v1.description:
Predefined conditions

IS_CLOUD_CONNECTED  : Condition will be true when reader is successfully connected to Cloud.

~IS_CLOUD_CONNECTED : Condition will be true when reader lost Cloud connectivity.

IS_RADIO_ONGOING    : Condition will be true when reader is currenty performing inventory or started inventory operation.

~IS_RADIO_ONGOING   : Condition will be true when reader is not performing inventory or stoped inventory operation.

operator.description: operator tobe used for conditions validation

actionConditions.v1.title: Action Conditions
actionConditions.v1.description:
Predefined conditions to trigger GPO or LED actions

Default "and" operator is used when array of conditions is provided.

operator "and": Action is performed only when all the conditions provided is true.
operator "or": Action is performed only when one of the conditions provided is true.

gpoAction.v1.title: GPO Action
gpoAction.v1.description:
Configuration to define operations on single GPO.

GPO Action will be skipped if conditions provided is not satisfied.

postActionState.description: Desired GPO pin state after action execution completed

ledAction.v1.title: LED Action
ledAction.v1.description:
Configuration to define operations on single LED.

LED Action will be skipped if conditions provided is not satisfied.

postActionColor.description: Desired LED color after action expired

gpoledAction.v1.title: GPO-LED Action
gpoledAction.v1.description: Array of GPO or LED actions. Actions will be performed sequentially.

GPIOLEDConfig.v1.title: GPIO-LED configuration
GPIOLEDConfig.v1.description:
GPIO-LED module configuration

Configure GPO and LED behaviour based on Events generated

Supported Events:
1. GPI_1_L: event raised when GPI 1 changed from HIGH to LOW
2. GPI_1_H: event raised when GPI 1 changed from LOW to HIGH
3. GPI_2_L: event raised when GPI 2 changed from HIGH to LOW
4. GPI_2_H: event raised when GPI 2 changed from LOW to HIGH
5. CLOUD_DISCONNECT: event raised when reader is disconnected from cloud
6. CLOUD_CONNECT: event raised when reader is connected to cloud
7. TAG_READ: event raised when a tag is read
8. RADIO_START: event raised when inventory operation started using START API
9. RADIO_STOP: event raised when inventory operation stopped using STOP API

1.description: set default state for GPO pin 1

2.description: set default state for GPO pin 2

3.description: set default state for GPO pin 3

4.description: set default state for GPO pin 4

retention.title: Tag Data Events Retention Configuration
retention.description: Tag Data Events Retention Configuration on connection lost

throttle.description: Rate (in events per second) to report data events when network is reconnected

maxNumEvents.description: Maximum number of events to retain

maxEventRetentionTimeInMin.description:
Maximum event retention time (in minutes)

0 or -1 indicate Events will be retained Forever.
-1 is the default

Example:
10 indicate Events generated in last 10 minutes will be retained

reportingInterval.description: Event Report interval in milliseconds. -1 disables this option

maxPayloadSizePerReport.description: Maximum payload size in bytes. -1 disables

radioPacketLog.description: Enables or Disables the Radio Control radio packet log
